reasoning: The question asks if there will be 8+ Fed rate cuts (which equals 200 basis points) in 2025. Based on FOMC reports through October 2025, the Federal Reserve started 2025 with the target funds rate at 4.25%-4.50%. In September 2025, they cut the rate by 25 bps to 4.00%-4.25%. In October 2025, they made a second cut of 25 bps, bringing the rate to 3.75%-4.00%. This means there have only been 2 rate cuts (50 bps total) so far in 2025. With only the December meeting left in the year, the Fed would have to cut rates by an unprecedented 150 basis points (6 "cuts" equivalent) in a single meeting to reach 8 total cuts. Given this mathematical reality and the absence of any massive financial collapse right now, 8+ cuts in 2025 is effectively impossible. The prediction market correctly corroborates this by pricing the chance at 0.00. Therefore, I assign the lowest possible probability of 0.02.
